subject: Mathematics (NCERT - Ganita Prakash)
chapter: Another Peek Beyond the Point (Decimals - Multiplication and Division)

activities_projects.title: The Equalizer (Decimal by Decimal)
activities_projects.objective: Divide a decimal by another decimal by making the divisor a whole number
steps: Take a problem like $1.25 \div 0.5$
Multiply both numbers by 10 to clear the decimal from the divisor ($12.5 \div 5$)
Perform the division and record the quotient
Try another: $0.004 \div 0.02$ and explain which power of 10 you chose
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Masters the technique of shifting decimals to simplify division
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct conversion and final quotient (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Area of a Postage Stamp
activities_projects.objective: Calculate the area of a rectangle with two decimal sides
steps: Draw a rectangle with Length = 3.2 cm and Width = 2.4 cm
Calculate the area using the formula $L 	imes W$
Count the total decimal places in the factors and ensure the product matches
Repeat with a square of side 1.5 cm
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Correctly applies decimal multiplication to spatial measurements
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in calculating area with correct units ($cm^2$) (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Average Lap Time
activities_projects.objective: Find the mean of decimal values
steps: Record the time taken to write a sentence 4 times (e.g., 5.4s, 6.2s, 5.8s, 6.6s)
Add the total time using decimal addition
Divide the total by 4 to find the average time
Round the answer to one decimal place
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Integrates decimal addition and division to find representative values
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the mean calculation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Currency Converter
steps: Imagine you have $10.50$
Multiply your dollars by the exchange rate to find the value in Rupees
Explain how you handled the two decimal places in the multiplier and the multiplicand
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Uses decimal multiplication for economic calculations
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct use of the decimal point in the final product (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Science of Rainfall
activities_projects.objective: Divide a total decimal by a whole number to find daily rates
steps: If a city receives 15.75 cm of rain over 7 days, find the average daily rainfall
If the rainfall doubles next week, calculate the new total and new daily average
Represent the daily rainfall on a simple line graph
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies decimal division to environmental data
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct daily average and graph representation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Fuel Efficiency Trip
activities_projects.objective: Calculate distance using fuel consumption rates
steps: A car travels 18.5 km using 1 liter of petrol
Calculate how far it can go with 3.5 liters
Calculate how many liters are needed for a 74 km journey using division
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Solves multi-step problems involving both multiplication and division of decimals
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical flow and correct answers (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Decimal Pattern Detective
activities_projects.objective: Predict outcomes of repeated decimal multiplication
steps: Multiply $0.1 	imes 0.1$, then $0.1 	imes 0.1 	imes 0.1$, and then $0.1 	imes 0.1 	imes 0.1 	imes 0.1$
Observe the number of zeros after the decimal point in each step
Write a rule to predict the product of $0.1$ multiplied by itself 6 times
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes exponential patterns in decimal placement
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the pattern rule (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Recipe Resizer
activities_projects.objective: Adjust ingredient quantities using decimal multipliers
materials: A simple recipe card (e.g., 2.5 cups of flour for 1 cake)
steps: Calculate the ingredients needed for 1.5 cakes
Calculate the ingredients needed for 0.5 (half) a cake
Check your work by adding the 1.0 cake and 0.5 cake amounts
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies decimal multiplication to scaling quantities

activities_projects.title: Division by $0.1$ and $0.01$
activities_projects.objective: Understand the inverse effect of dividing by small decimals
steps: Compare these results with $5 	imes 10$, $5 	imes 100$, and $5 	imes 1000$
Write a conclusion about what happens to a number when divided by a decimal less than 1
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Discovery of the relationship between decimal division and whole number multiplication

activities_projects.title: Decimal Price Tag Duel
steps: Bottle A: 1.5 Liters for ₹90.00; Bottle B: 0.5 Liters for ₹35.50
Divide price by volume for both to find the price per 1 Liter
Determine which bottle is the 'better buy' and by how much
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Uses decimal division for smart consumer decision-making
